author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
title: The Same Continued: 'He Reveals Wisdom.'

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But what shall I say about the revelation of wisdom?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For there is no man who can in the present life very well hope to attain to the great revelations which were given to the Apostle Paul; and of course it is impossible to suppose that anything was accustomed in these revelations to be made known to him but what appertained to wisdom.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Yet for all this he says: “Lest I should be exalted above measure through the abundance of the revelations, there was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: For this thing I besought the Lord thrice, that He would take it away from me.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And He said unto me, My grace is sufficient for thee; for my strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now, undoubtedly, if there were already in the apostle that perfection of love which admitted of no further addition, and which could be puffed up no more, there could have been no further need of the messenger of Satan to buffet him, and thereby to repress the excessive elation which might arise from abundance of revelations.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: What means this elation, however, but a being puffed up?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: And of love it has been indeed most truly said, “Love vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: This love, therefore, was still in process of constant increase in the great apostle, day by day, as long as his “inward man was renewed day by day,” and would then be perfected, no doubt, when he was got beyond the reach of all further vaunting and elation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: But at that time his mind was still in a condition to be inflated by an abundance of revelations before it was perfected in the solid edifice of love; for he had not arrived at the goal and apprehended the prize, to which he was reaching forward in his course.
